title: Eravikulangara Temple
tagline: Eravikulangara Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Eravikulangara Bhagavathy Temple is an ancient Hindu temple in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India, dedicated to Bhagavan Sivan and Bhagavan Ayyappan.
overview.description: Eravikulangara Bhagavathy Temple is an ancient Hindu temple in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India, dedicated to Bhagavan Sivan and Bhagavan Ayyappan.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Akaparambu
topPlaces.description: Akaparambu, or Akapparambu is a village in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India, near the Cochin International Airport and south of the town of Angamaly.

topPlaces.name: Athanasius Paulose II
topPlaces.description: Athanasius Paulose (27 June 1915 – 7 March 1991) was the First Metropolitan of Evangelistic Association of the East. He was consecrated as Metropolitan in 1973.

topPlaces.name: Athani, Ernakulam
topPlaces.description: Athani is a village in Nedumbasery Panchayat in Aluva Taluk. Athani is between Angamaly and Aluva in Eranakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: CIAL Solar Power Project
topPlaces.description: The CIAL Solar Power Project is a 50 megawatt (MW) photovoltaic power station built at Cochin International Airport, India, by the company Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL). Cochin International Airport became the first fully solar powered airport in the world with the commissioning the plant.

topPlaces.name: Neduvannoor
topPlaces.description: Neduvannoor is a village situated near Cochin International Airport in Ernakulam District in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kaprassery
topPlaces.description: Kaprassery is a village in the district of Ernakulam and is situated on the National Highway 544 (India) (Old NH47) that stretches from Salem to Kochi. It is located between the two major towns of the Indian state Kerala, namely, Aluva and Angamaly.

topPlaces.name: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple
topPlaces.description: Thiru Nayathode Siva Narayana Temple is located at Nayathod, a small village in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. The temple is located at about 3 km from Cochin International Airport.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Jacobite Soonoro Cathedral, Angamaly
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Jacobite Syrian Soonoro Cathedral, or Angamaly Cheriyapally, is an ancient Jacobite Syrian church located in Angamaly.

image.alt: St. Mary's Jacobite Soonoro Cathedral, Angamaly

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Eravikulangara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Eravikulangara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Eravikulangara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (3 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Eravikulangara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Eravikulangara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Eravikulangara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Eravikulangara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Eravikulangara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Akaparambu, Athanasius Paulose II, Athani, Ernakulam, CIAL Solar Power Project, Neduvannoor.
